steps.title: Find total composition from muscle percentage
steps.content:
We know muscle is 142 kg and comprises 67.0% of the musk ox's total composition. Using the percentage formula:
$$\text{Total} = \frac{142}{0.670} \approx 211.94 \text{ kg}$$

steps.title: Calculate the remaining composition
steps.content:
Since muscle makes up 67.0%, the remaining composition (bone and fat combined) is 100% - 67.0% = 33.0% of the total:
$$\text{Remaining} = 211.94 \times 0.330 \approx 69.94 \text{ kg}$$

steps.title: Find bone mass from the remaining composition
steps.content:
Of the remaining composition, 43.6% is bone:
$$\text{Bone} = 69.94 \times 0.436 \approx 30.49 \text{ kg}$$
Rounded to the nearest tenth: 30.5 kg
